category_title: Students internalizing failure from negative teacher feedback
minimal_context: The student is working on an English paper using a binder of materials for the year.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your binder and continue working on your English paper.

STUDENT: I was working on a paper and I couldn’t—I didn’t get it.

TUTOR: Try to review the instructions again and see what you can do.

STUDENT: I'm trying to talk to you that I didn't get it, to help me out.

TUTOR: You need to figure it out yourself. If you just stop trying, you're going to fail anyways.

STUDENT: (Closes the binder) Fine, then I'm just a failure. I'm completely soured on this class and I'm leaving.

options.A: Provide frequent reminders and cues while the student works on the English paper.
options.B: Interact informally, "pal around" with everyone in the class, and treat the student like an equal.
options.C: Deliver opportunities to respond consistently while the student works on the English paper.
options.D: Terminate demands and remove the binder for 10 seconds contingent upon the challenging behavior.
